danger_signals.visual_form: Flying sprites moving in predetermined or aggressive patterns toward player
danger_signals.meaning: Active threats requiring evasion and destruction for credits

danger_signals.visual_form: Stationary defensive structures with aiming behavior
danger_signals.meaning: Localized threat zones with directional fire patterns

danger_signals.visual_form: Large-scale enemy structures appearing at wave endpoints or mid-wave in later difficulties
danger_signals.meaning: Major threat escalation; modular destruction required

danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy units on direct collision course with player sprite
Inbound fire from turrets and aircraft
danger_signals.meaning: Passive damage threat; can be cleared by megabomb except during boss phases

safety_signals.visual_form: Regular shield pool (100 points) regenerating when player ceases fire
safety_signals.meaning: Player durability increases during non-engagement windows

safety_signals.visual_form: Purchasable secondary shield barrier (up to 5 stacked layers)
safety_signals.meaning: Accumulated defense investment provides buffer against catastrophic damage

safety_signals.visual_form: Visual rewards from destroyed enemies, proportional to enemy toughness
safety_signals.meaning: Economic progress toward survival upgrades; incentivizes enemy engagement

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible weapons spawned or purchased before/after waves
safety_signals.meaning: Tactical advantage gain; enables category-specific targeting (air vs ground)
Emergency reset button for overwhelming threat situations

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Military bases, oil infrastructure, refineries, airfields

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Military installations, oil processing facilities, coastal rig platforms
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Oil silos, tank farms, petroleum refinery, offshore drilling rig

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Industrial, agricultural, and urban military infrastructure
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Chemical plants, farmland, jungle temples, urban cityscape, military airbase
Lunar bases, Mars-like terrain, ice world installations, volcanic compounds, space station

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Generic barren terrain with occasional forests and rivers; coastal to inland progression; oil/petroleum industry-focused aesthetics
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Lower enemy density; basic military and industrial targets; straightforward level progression

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Thematically diverse environments cycling through water/chemical plants, farmland, jungle, city, and airbase; distinct visual identity per level; night-time city level with illumination
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Increased enemy variety and complexity; boss encounters scale in difficulty; mid-wave boss insertions in later levels

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Extraplanetary and space settings: lunar body with dark-side night variant, red Mars-analog, ice world, volcanic world, orbital space station
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Modular ground-based bosses requiring component destruction; shift from industrial to extraterrestrial threat paradigm

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Same environmental assets with intensified threat density
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Difficulty tier progression (Rookie → Veteran → Elite); Elite mode removes shield regeneration; boss reappearance at wave midpoints in harder runs

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Shop menu access with accumulated credits; visible weapon/shield portfolio expansion
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Weapon loadout switching; phase shield layer stacking; dynamic weapon selection during combat

environmental_storytelling.example: Bravo Sector establishes petroleum/military industrial complex (oil silos, refineries, rigs); Tango introduces civilian/urban targets (cities, farmland, temples); Outer Regions expands to multi-planetary corporate competition
Night waves in Tango Sector (illuminated city) and Outer Regions (lunar dark side) create atmosphere shift and threat density change within thematic continuity
Coastal-to-inland movement in Bravo Sector suggests systematic takeover; jungle-to-city in Tango implies expanding footprint; planetary migration in Outer Regions indicates corporate scale escalation

Industrial modular structures in Outer Regions suggest mechanized corporate entities; large aircraft boss in Tango suggests military infrastructure; rig platforms in Bravo signal resource extraction focus
Mix of flying enemies, turrets, ground vehicles, and buildings creates layered threat ecosystem reflecting populated/defended territories

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Predetermined path movement; some attempt collision ramming; destroyed for credit reward proportional to threat level
Stationary aim-and-fire targeting; continuous threat during approach phase; destroyable for credits
Large health pool; may appear mid-wave in higher difficulties; modular destruction (separate targetable components); megabomb reduces but does not eliminate except at max damage
Direct vector toward player; high damage on collision; indicates desperation or designed suicide tactics
Rapid shield regeneration when not firing; capable of sustained engagement
Shield regeneration slows significantly; vulnerability to cascading damage; phase shield layers absorb subsequent hits
No shield regeneration; reliance on phase layers and evasion; increased threat perception

notes: Raptor uses implicit factionalism: MegaCorps employs the player as mercenary against competing corporations and entities across three escalating sectors. World-state progression is signaled primarily through environmental theming rather than explicit narrative. Danger scales with sector progression (generic industrial → diverse infrastructure → extraplanetary) and difficulty mode. Safety mechanics (regenerating shields, weapon variety, megabomb) create resource management tension against economic incentive (credits proportional to damage taken). No faction visual identity system (colors, sigils) is documented; distinction relies entirely on environmental context and target architecture. The 'night wave' mechanic serves as world-state marker for threat intensity and thematic variation within sectors.
